title: University AI Policy Tracker — QS 2026 Ranks 26-30
summary: This staged report covers AI-related policies and guidance at five universities ranked QS 2026 positions 26-30: Australian National University (ANU), Seoul National University (SNU), Johns Hopkins University (JHU), University of Tokyo (UTokyo), and Columbia University. Across 42 source candidates and 40 source snapshots, 90 claims were extracted and reviewed. 76 claims are approved as agent-reviewed staged findings; 14 claims require evidence rebinding before publication. ANU, SNU, and UTokyo contain the strongest directly evidenced material in this run. JHU and Columbia discovery found relevant official AI-policy sources, but several findings were downgraded because their evidence was based on search snippets or source-verification notes, and some pages involved Cloudflare/WAF plus Firecrawl fallback. This staged dataset is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

14 claims are marked needs_changes and must not be published as canonical findings until evidence is rebound to direct source text: claim-columbia-001a, claim-columbia-002a, claim-columbia-003a, claim-columbia-006a, claim-columbia-008a, claim-jhu-001a, claim-jhu-002a, claim-jhu-003a, claim-jhu-004a, claim-jhu-005a, claim-jhu-006a, claim-jhu-008a, claim-snu-001a, claim-u-tokyo-005a.

JHU and Columbia include source candidates discovered through OpenCLI search where some URLs were affected by Cloudflare/WAF and Firecrawl fallback. Claims relying on search snippets or verification notes have been downgraded to needs_changes.
2 PDF sources could not be fetched: cand-jhu-007 (JHU Press Generative AI Policy for Authors) and cand-u-tokyo-007 (UTokyo student document AI policy).
Multilingual sources: SNU sources are primarily in Korean; UTokyo sources are primarily in Japanese with English translations available for some policy documents.
Source coverage varies by university and should be treated as staged coverage pending human review and future canonical ingestion.
sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
